meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Beginner's Guide for Student Developers
content.seo_description: Break through coding blocks with AI. Master debugging using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your portfolio building and launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code into your workflow, you transform debugging from a manual struggle into an automated diagnostic process. This checklist provides the exact prompts and CLI workflows to isolate errors, refactor legacy code, and ship features faster.
content.intro:
The screen is dark, the cursor blinks with rhythmic indifference, and your terminal is bleeding red error messages. You’ve been stuck on a single bug for hours, watching your momentum evaporate as frustration sets in. This is the 'beginner’s wall'—that invisible barrier where most students abandon their projects, convinced they lack the 'natural talent' to code.

But here is the secret: engineering isn't about knowing every syntax rule; it's about mastering the feedback loop. When you stop fighting the compiler and start treating AI as your senior pair programmer, the wall crumbles. It is time to stop guessing and start building with precision.

steps.title: Contextualize the Environment
steps.description: Before debugging, you must provide the AI with the current state of your environment to avoid hallucinated solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify potential dependency conflicts in my package.json.

steps.title: Isolate the Stack Trace
steps.description: Don't paste entire files. Extract the specific error and the function context to keep the AI focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this stack trace and map it to the specific lines of code in the offending component.

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Generate tests to confirm the bug is fixed and prevent regression in your growing codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that recreates this specific bug, then suggest the fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for this module'

steps.title: Refactor for Readability
steps.description: Once the bug is squashed, have the AI refactor the code to adhere to clean code principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic using clean code principles and add JSDoc comments for maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor this function for better performance'

steps.title: Review and Commit
steps.description: Use the AI to write a professional commit message that documents your learning process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise, professional Git commit message for these changes following conventional commits.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m "$(claude run 'write a commit message for these changes')"

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to 'explain the logic of the fix' rather than just providing the code; this builds your mental model.
Use Claude Code to iterate on UI components by describing the visual state rather than the CSS implementation.
If the AI gives a wrong answer, tell it 'You are hallucinating, re-read the file' to force a deeper context scan.
Keep your project directory clean; AI performs significantly better when there are no unused, conflicting legacy files.
Treat your AI interactions as a code review process—always question its suggestions before merging to production.
content.next_steps: Once your bug is resolved, push your code to GitHub, write a brief 'Post-Mortem' blog post explaining what you learned, and start on your next feature module immediately.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap to stop being a student and start being an engineer. Every bug you squash is a lesson that brings you closer to your first production-ready application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
